approved_answer: Rail A — UHI insurance package (insured persons): If someone is registered as “insured” in the UHI registry, they can use the insurance package, whose services and medicines are state-defined and can involve co-pay rules and drug reimbursement caps. The key point: once insured (whether the premium is paid by the person/employer or by the state), the person’s coverage is not “whatever their employer decided”; it is a state-defined insurance package.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception
source_reference: Eligibility for state-supported healthcare and medicine benefits in Armenia.pdf

approved_answer: Law on Universal Health Insurance (ՀՀ օրենքը «Առողջության համընդհանուր ապահովագրության մասին», HO-459-N, adopted 17.12.2025; published 24.12.2025). Defines who 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 • 1 must have UHI, the population groups, “insured” vs “uninsured,” phased implementation dates, drug reimbursement principles, and complaint processes. Government Decision N 1974-N (25.12.2025): Defines the minimum volume and additional services for uninsured persons, the lists of statuses/groups who can access those additional services, and related operational rules, including a state-paid package for certain “social package” beneficiaries and certain workers.

subtopic: Core definitions that control the logic
patterns: Core definitions that control the logic
core definitions that control the logic
approved_answer: Insured person: someone included in one of the population groups defined by Law Article 11(2) and holding the required registry status at the time of the insured event. Uninsured person: not included in Law Article 11(2) groups 1–15 and without a UHI insurance package. For uninsured citizens, the state guarantees a minimum volume of services, plus additional services only while a specific status exists; the government defines the minimum/additional lists and eligible statuses. approved_answer: You can safely do these: - Explain what “insured” vs “uninsured” means and that insured status is registry- based. - Explain which population groups are in Law Article 11(2), and that phases exist (some groups are active in 2026, others scheduled later). - Explain that uninsured citizens have a state-paid minimum service volume and that additional services exist only for certain statuses (and list those statuses). - Explain that medicine benefits exist for listed groups and listed diseases, with full or partial reimbursement. - Explain that notifications about UHI status and payment obligations can be sent via eHealth/ARMED, email, SMS, official email, or via providers.

approved_answer: Escalate immediately when: - The user asks “Am I covered?” about a specific person (themselves, parent, child) and expects a yes/no. You can map them to the likely group, but final confirmation requires registry check (UHI registry + underlying source registries). - The user’s situation depends on facts you cannot verify: residency status, employment wage threshold, whether employer is a tax agent, whether the person is registered/identified in the vulnerability system, whether disability status has been recorded or transitioned, etc. - The question becomes medical-case dependent: “Do I qualify for free meds for my condition?” “Will this surgery be paid?

The Prime Minister’s official communication explicitly pushed citizens to use the updated ARMED app to understand their status. If status is unclear: contact Universal Health Insurance Fund via its official contact line (8866) or official email (info@uhif.am).

Social vulnerability status: Government Decision 1978-N states that information about vulnerable family members is provided through the unified social protection information system via the interoperability platform and that identification uses public services number or ID document data. If 65 • 66 • 67 • 15 • 14 13 14 49 12 12 • 10 the UHI registry shows missing vulnerability status, the likely correction is in the social protection data pipeline.

if a citizen believes a clinic wrongly refused them
approved_answer: There are two distinct complaint paths: 1) Registry/status/payment dispute → UHI Fund verification and correction first (because services are tied to status). 2) Insurance benefit/coverage dispute → the UHI law contains a structured complaint framework (written complaints, review timelines, and a designated oversight/complaint body), and it also requires a mandatory mediation step before court for certain disputes over reduced/denied insurance compensation.

approved_answer: These are short “knowledge cards” that can be used verbatim in an assistant, with built-in escalation points. Card: Working coverage - Rule: In 2026, “I work” does not automatically mean you are insured under UHI yet; Armenia’s UHI is phased, and early phase-in depends on legally defined payer categories and income/ filing thresholds (notably the 200,001 AMD+ wage-trigger rules). - Safe next step: Check status in ARMED or contact the UHI Fund hotline 8866. - Escalate if: the person needs a yes/no for a specific clinic visit today → requires registry check. Card: Children - Rule: Children under 18 (and specific “without parental care” extensions) are explicit UHI population groups.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception
source_reference: Eligibility for state-supported healthcare and medicine benefits in Armenia.pdf

approved_answer: The Universal Health Insurance (UHI) system is established by the Law “On Universal Health Insurance” (2025)[1]. This law (adopted 17 Dec 2025) and implementing government decrees define the insurance package and operations. In Dec 2025 the government approved the final insurance package and reimbursable services list and created the UHI Fund[1][2].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The UHI is a transitional, phased system. Its first phase launched on 1 Jan 2026[3]. Plans foresee gradual expansion over 3 years (e.g. health minister stated “in 3 years by 2028 the entire population will be insured”[4]). Thus UHI is active (in first phase) and transitional.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: UHI is a state-subsidized universal framework implemented in phases. Initially, only targeted groups are insured (with full state subsidy)[5][6]; ultimately all citizens/residents will be included. In effect it is a phased universal model – starting with priority groups and then extending to the general population (coverage of rural residents, students, etc by 2028)[5][6].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Insurance covers RA citizens and legal residents meeting the law’s criteria. By law, all citizens/residents in one of the listed groups are insured[7][8]. Key first-phase groups (2026) include: children (0–18), the elderly (65+), persons with disabilities (groups 1–3) or severe functional limitations, war veterans and their families (including combatants since 2016 and fallen-soldier families), large/foster families, and socio-economically vulnerable persons on social assistance[7].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: UHI is mandatory. Who pays: Employers and employers’ tax agents must withhold and pay the insurance premium from employees’ wages; registered self-employed persons (sole-proprietors, notaries, etc.) pay their own premiums[8]. Collection: Premiums are collected via the payroll tax system. For example, an employer must withhold UHI contributions from any employee who earned over AMD 200,000 in a given month, and remit it by the 20th of the next month[11][12]. Tax-exempt employers’ employees pay and remit their own contributions directly[13]. Mandatory vs. voluntary: It is compulsory for all qualifying persons.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The state fully subsidizes (pays) the premiums for the priority groups listed in law (children, elderly, disabled, veterans, socially vulnerable, etc.)[7]. These groups are therefore 100% subsidized. All other insured persons (e.g. regular workers) must pay their own premiums. ⚠ Partially Specified: The law does not mention any partial subsidies or sliding scales. (In practice, the government has subsidized most of the premium for lower-income earners in 2026, but such arrangements are not codified in law.)
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: All insurance funds (premiums) flow into the state’s Universal Health Insurance Fund (UHIF), a government-established non-profit body[16][17]. The UHIF signs contracts with healthcare providers and pharmacies on behalf of insured persons[17]. It then reimburses those providers for covered services: by law the Fund “pays insurance compensations” to medical facilities for services rendered under the insurance package[18]. In effect, employers remit contributions to the State, and funds are allocated to the UHIF, which dispenses payments to providers.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The UHI insurance package covers a broad array of medically necessary services. By government decision, the package includes primary (ambulatory) care, outpatient specialists, hospital (inpatient) care, diagnostic tests, and a defined list of medicines[2][19]. (See also government app listings of covered services.) In short, if a service or drug is in the approved insurance package, the UHIF reimburses the provider (often with co-payments for patients). Preventive and general practitioner services are fully covered (patients book a free visit to a GP or pediatrician as the “gatekeeper”[20]).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The law provides that failure to pay required premiums triggers tax enforcement. Non-payment is handled by tax authorities as if the contribution were a tax debt[21]. (The law does not specify criminal or other sanctions beyond tax collection.) If premiums are unpaid, the individual loses the “insured” status as of the last day of that month[22]. The government has warned that failure to comply may lead to sanctions on providers, but no additional legal penalties for patients are defined.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: In practice, eligible citizens are entered into the national UHI registry. A person’s insured status can be checked via the ARMED e-health platform. To use benefits, an insured patient typically books a free primary-care visit and undergoes any preventive screening (all without out-of-pocket cost)[20]. The GP can then refer the patient to specialists or order tests as needed. When services are delivered, the provider bills the UHIF for reimbursement under the contract. Pharmacies dispense covered medicines (as per lists announced by the Ministry).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Emergency and urgent care remain universally available. By regulation, uninsured persons (including foreigners or those excluded from early phases) are guaranteed a minimum health package. In Dec 2025 the government defined the “basic package” for uninsured individuals[23]. For example, Annex 1 to Decision No.1974-N (25 Dec 2025) lists non-insured groups entitled to free care (e.g. pregnant women, conscripts, detainees, asylum seekers, trafficking victims, etc.)[24]. Annex 2/3 set out the minimum services for the uninsured. In practice, anyone not in the UHI scheme (e.g.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Transitional/Active. UHI is implemented for Phase 1 (as of Jan 2026) and expanding. It is not yet universal – rather it’s in a phased transition (first phase covers ~1.6 million people[26], with plans to add remaining groups by 2028[4][6]).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: State-Guaranteed Free Healthcare Package (SGFP) – the traditional system of publicly funded care for vulnerable groups.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Based on government decree 318-N (4 Mar 2004) on state-guaranteed free care[27] (and subsequent amendments). This decree (and related laws) mandates that certain groups receive fully state-funded medical services as part of annual “targeted health programs”.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Fully implemented (ongoing). This is the legacy system coexisting with UHI. It has been active since before 2026 and continues in parallel (gradually being absorbed into UHI over time[25]).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Targeted. Only specific vulnerable categories are entitled (via state programs). Coverage is not universal; it applies only to listed groups (see below). It is entirely funded from the state budget (through the Ministry of Health and other agencies)[28].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The decree defines eligibility via appendices. Annex 1 (see [29]) lists “socially vulnerable and special groups” entitled to free/preferential care. These typically include: recipients of social aid, large/low-income families, war veterans and families of fallen, disabled people, pensioners (some categories), and others (as updated periodically). In short, persons in that official list (Appendix 1) have state-guaranteed care[29]. (For example, pensioners, the very poor, veterans, homeless, etc., are covered under SGFP.)
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: N/A. There are no premiums or contributions. Beneficiaries of SGFP receive care at no cost; funding comes from the state budget[27].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: SGFP is entirely state-funded, so 100% of the cost for these groups is borne by the government. (These groups are effectively fully subsidized by definition.)
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Funds for SGFP come directly from the national budget. The Ministry of Health (and, for certain programs, the Ministry of Labor and Social Affairs) channel funds to hospitals and clinics. For example, annual budget allocations pay contracted providers to deliver the free services[28][30]. There is no separate insurance fund – payments are made out of the treasury under budget programs.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: SGFP covers a defined package of services for each program (preventive, ambulatory, inpatient, etc.). Under decree 318-N, “free medical care and services” are provided within state programs[28][30]. In practice, this has meant free primary care, hospitalization, medications, and interventions for those groups (for specific conditions and amounts as approved). The package content is not the same as the insurance package, but conceptually parallels it: eligible patients receive a set list of services at no charge, funded by the state.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: As a state obligation, there is no “compliance” issue for SGFP users. Providers must deliver guaranteed services to entitled persons. There are no penalties for beneficiaries (they simply use care as entitled).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Eligible persons present for care at public clinics/hospitals under the state programs. They are identified via social or clinical registration and treated under the state budgeted quotas. For example, a person on social assistance or a war pension registers at a polyclinic and receives care without paying. The process is administrative (state-issued “vouchers” or direct billing to the treasury), not insurance claims.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: SGFP only covers the groups on the official list. Persons outside those groups had to pay before UHI (or remain uninsured). Under the UHI transition, any gaps are filled by temporary measures (see UHI card). Emergency services for everyone have always been available as part of SGFP framework, and those continue (as specified by law) regardless of insurance.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Active. This legacy free-care system remains in effect for its target groups. It is being gradually supplanted by UHI (with budget programs “phased out” over 3 years)[31][25], but as of early 2026 it is still operational.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Contributions (insurance premiums) are mandated by the UHI law. The law’s Article 11 explicitly includes wage earners, self-employed, and other income recipients as “insured persons” (and thus as premium payers)[8]. Government regulations specify payment procedures.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The contribution system is implemented (as of Jan 2026) and applies immediately to those eligible. It is an integral part of the transitional UHI system.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Primarily employers (via tax agents) and self-employed individuals. All salaried employees (earning above threshold) are insured by virtue of their employment, and their employers must withhold the UHI premium from wages[8]. Individual entrepreneurs, sole proprietors, notaries, etc., pay directly.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Through the payroll/tax system. Employers calculate and deduct the premium from each eligible employee’s salary and remit it monthly to the State (initially into the national budget)[11]. For 2025 transition, December 2025 earnings were used to calculate initial premiums (remitted by Jan 20, 2026)[11]. From January 2026 onward, employers remit by the 20th of each following month for the prior month’s wages[12]. If the employer is not a tax agent (tax-exempt), the employee themselves pays the contribution to the state[13].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Contributions are mandatory for all persons in covered categories. No voluntary opt-in is offered.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Persons in the state-subsidized categories pay zero contributions (the state pays on their behalf)[14][7]. Specifically, employees under age 18 or over 65 have no premium withheld[14]. Also, income earned under civil labor contracts and similar (which are not taxed as wages) is exempt[14]. Persons not in the workforce or not meeting insurance criteria simply do not pay (but they are also not insured until covered by future phases).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The law does not specify flat rates, but official guidelines fix them. As of 2026, two monthly premium levels apply: AMD 4,800 for salaries up to AMD 500,000, and AMD 10,800 for salaries above AMD 500,000[15]. (All employees pay a fixed monthly sum, regardless of salary beyond the threshold.) ⚠ Contribution rates are not explicitly set in the law (the law allows the government to set amounts); the above figures come from government/tax authority clarifications[15].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Non-payment is handled by the tax authority. Late or unpaid contributions are treated as tax arrears (see Enforcement). An employee whose salary is insufficient to cover the premium (or who fails to pay) loses insured status (per law)[22]. In practice, an employer who fails to withhold faces tax penalties under fiscal law.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The categories of “insured persons” are defined by the UHI law (Article 11, Clause 2)[7][8]. This law entry sets exact eligibility conditions.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Transitional (phased). Only specified groups are currently insured; coverage expands over time.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Targeted-by-category (phase 1), ultimately universal. Initially, only high-priority groups are eligible (with state subsidy). Over time the coverage widens to include all formal wage earners and eventually all residents[5][6].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: To be insured, a person must be an RA citizen or resident and belong to one of the groups listed in the law. These include (among others)[7][8]:
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Under age 18 (plus orphans up to age 26)[7].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Persons with significant functional limitations or disability Groups 1–3[7].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Of minors or disabled children (within specified conditions)[7].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Veterans of national defense (e.g. Nagorno-Karabakh), fallen-soldier families, and participants in certain combat operations[7].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Families in state social support programs (e.g. low-income families above a points threshold)[32].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Any person earning wages (where taxes are withheld)[8], registered entrepreneurs/notaries[8], and those receiving certain non-labor income (rent, dividends, civil contract work)[33].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Individuals in 11–14 who are also engaged in farming (as specified)[34]. If a person falls into multiple categories, the most favorable status applies.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The law explicitly covers RA citizens and foreign residents (with legal status) who meet the above criteria. Refugees and asylum-seekers are excluded[35] (they are covered by separate state schemes).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: By law, all formally employed and self-employed persons are insured (and must pay) regardless of salary (the law does not carve out low incomes)[8]. (In practice, an income threshold was applied in the rollout, but it is a policy decision rather than a legal eligibility test.)
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The law’s detailed list (points 1–10) ensures that recognized vulnerable categories are automatically insured (with state-paid premiums)[7]. For example, persons caring for multiple children or families of fallen soldiers are covered as a matter of right.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: The law does not detail certain administrative criteria (e.g. how long one must reside in RA). Residency requirements are assumed (e.g. >183 days/yr) but not spelled out in UHI law. The law also does not mention the specific income cut-off (the “>200,000 AMD” rule was set by regulation in 2025). Until explicitly enacted, these remain government policy details (⚠ not legally specified).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Insured status is granted by law as soon as conditions are met. Conversely, if one ceases to belong to a covered group (e.g. no longer employed or loses residency), insured status ends at month-end[22]. This provides a clear cause-and-effect for eligibility.
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Transitional/Active. UHI eligibility is law, but only partially implemented. Phase 1 groups are actively insured as of 2026 (verification via registries)[5]. By late 2025, about 1.006 million people were included in these categories[36]. Remaining groups will be added in phases (2027–2028).
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: Armenian legislation and government documents[1][16][2][7][15][19]; Ministry press and official communications[5][4][6][23]; Universal Health Insurance Fund materials[37][20].
escalate_if: User asks for personal status confirmation or exact current registry result

approved_answer: 65 և բարձր տարիքը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ որոշ պետական առողջապահական ծրագրերի և արտոնությունների դեպքում։

field_questions.benefit_type: Ո՞ր հարցի մասին է խոսքը՝ բուժօգնությո՞ւն, դեղորա՞յք, թե հետազոտությո՞ւն։

answer_rules.answer: 65 և բարձր տարիքը կարող է հաշվի առնվել պետական ծրագրերով բուժօգնության որոշ տեսակների դեպքում, բայց վերջնական ծածկույթը կախված է ծառայությունից և ծրագրից։ Եթե պետք է ճշտել կոնկրետ դեպքը, զանգահարեք 8003։
65 և բարձր տարիքը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ դեղորայքային արտոնությունների դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է կոնկրետ դեղից և ծրագրից։ Եթե ուզում եք, կարող եք նշել դեղի անունը։
65 և բարձր տարիքը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ որոշ հետազոտությունների պետական ծածկույթի դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և ծրագրից։ Եթե ուզում եք, նշեք հետազոտության տեսակը։

escalate_if: Requires exact personal entitlement or live verification
source_reference: Eligibility for state-supported healthcare and medicine benefits in Armenia.pdf

approved_answer: Թոշակառու կարգավիճակը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ որոշ պետական առողջապահական ծրագրերի և արտոնությունների դեպքում։

field_questions.benefit_type: Ի՞նչն եք ուզում ճշտել՝ բուժօգնությո՞ւն, դեղորա՞յք, թե հետազոտությո՞ւն։

answer_rules.answer: Թոշակառու կարգավիճակը կարող է կարևոր լինել բուժօգնության որոշ պետական ծրագրերի դեպքում, բայց վերջնական ծածկույթը կախված է ծառայության տեսակից և կիրառվող ծրագրից։
Թոշակառու կարգավիճակը կարող է կարևոր լինել որոշ դեղորայքային արտոնությունների դեպքում, բայց պետք է հաշվի առնել նաև դեղի տեսակը և ծրագրային պայմանները։
Թոշակառու կարգավիճակը կարող է հաշվի առնվել որոշ հետազոտությունների ծածկույթի դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության և ծրագրի պայմաններից։

field_questions.benefit_type: Ո՞ր հարցն եք ուզում ճշտել՝ բուժօգնությո՞ւն, դեղորա՞յք, թե հետազոտությո՞ւն։

answer_rules.answer: Հաշմանդամության կարգավիճակը կարող է կիրառվել բուժօգնության որոշ պետական ծրագրերի դեպքում, բայց վերջնական ծածկույթը կախված է ծառայության և ծրագրի պայմաններից։
Հաշմանդամության կարգավիճակը կարող է կարևոր լինել որոշ դեղորայքային արտոնությունների համար, բայց վերջնականը կախված է կոնկրետ դեղից և ծրագրից։
Հաշմանդամության կարգավիճակը կարող է կարևոր լինել որոշ հետազոտությունների պետական ծածկույթի դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և ծրագրից։

approved_answer: Հղիությունը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ մայրական և որոշ առողջապահական ծառայությունների պետական ծածկույթի դեպքում։

field_questions.benefit_type: Ի՞նչ եք ուզում ճշտել՝ հետազոտությո՞ւն, բուժօգնությո՞ւն, թե այլ ծառայությո՞ւն։

answer_rules.answer: Հղիությունը կարող է հիմք լինել որոշ պետական ծածկույթների համար, հատկապես մայրական առողջության հետ կապված ծառայությունների դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է ծառայության տեսակից։
Հղիության դեպքում որոշ հետազոտություններ և սքրինինգային ծառայություններ կարող են պետական ծածկույթ ունենալ, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և ծրագրից։
Հղիության դեպքում պետական ծածկույթը հիմնականում պետք է նայել ծառայության կոնկրետ տեսքով։ Ասեք՝ որ ծառայության մասին է խոսքը, և կփորձեմ ընդհանուր կանոնը ասել։

answer_rules.answer: Սոցիալապես անապահով կարգավիճակը կարող է հիմք լինել որոշ բուժօգնության պետական ծածկույթի համար, բայց վերջնականը կախված է ծառայության տեսակից և ծրագրից։
Սոցիալապես անապահով կարգավիճակը կարող է հիմք լինել որոշ դեղորայքային արտոնությունների համար, բայց վերջնականը կախված է կոնկրետ դեղից և ծրագրից։
Սոցիալապես անապահով կարգավիճակը կարող է ազդեցություն ունենալ որոշ հետազոտությունների պետական ծածկույթի վրա, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և ծրագրից։

escalate_if: Requires exact personal entitlement or live verification
source_reference: Eligibility for state-supported healthcare and medicine benefits in Armenia.pdf

approved_answer: Աշխատող կամ ապահովագրված կարգավիճակը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ առողջապահական ծառայությունների ընդհանուր ծածկույթի դեպքում։

field_questions.benefit_type: Ի՞նչ եք ուզում ճշտել՝ բուժօգնությո՞ւն, դեղորա՞յք, թե հետազոտությո՞ւն։

answer_rules.answer: Աշխատող կամ ապահովագրված կարգավիճակը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ բուժօգնության ընդհանուր ծածկույթի դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է ծառայությունից և ծրագրային կանոններից։
Ապահովագրված կարգավիճակը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ որոշ դեղորայքային փոխհատուցումների դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է կոնկրետ դեղից և ծրագրից։
Ապահովագրված կարգավիճակը կարող է նշանակություն ունենալ որոշ հետազոտությունների ծածկույթի դեպքում, բայց վերջնականը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և ծրագրից։
